hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing regularization during training (such as equigrad) causes non-equivariant architectures to converge toward the same representational manifold as architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization during training will produce a monotonic increase in cross-architecture representational alignment that plateaus at a value indistinguishable from that between two fully equivariant architectures, providing a quantitative threshold at which learned symmetry constraints become architecturally redundant and enabling the identification of a minimal regularization budget sufficient to achieve foundation-level representational generality without the computational overhead of hard-coded equivariance.
